passage.title: Kitchen Job Trial
passage.text: Our family tested two ways to remember evening chores last month. Yuna used a plain paper list, but Theo used job cards on a ring. After five evenings, Theo missed fewer jobs because the ring cards were easy to carry. Our family marked each finished chore on a chart by the table. When Yuna saw the difference, she changed to job cards on a ring. By the weekend, she remembered more tasks by herself. The students talked about the test before choosing a better method. The group learned that small reminders can help a family share work better.

questions.prompt: What is the passage mostly about?
choices: Testing a clearer way to remember evening jobs
questions.rationale: The whole passage follows one test and what the group learned from it.

questions.prompt: What did Theo use to remember his jobs?
questions.rationale: The second sentence directly tells which tool or place was used.

questions.prompt: What did the group check or write down?
questions.rationale: The fourth sentence explains what the group checked or recorded.

questions.prompt: What does difference mean in the fifth sentence?
choices: A loud sound in a room
The way two things are not the same
questions.rationale: Difference means the two results did not match each other.

questions.prompt: What does chore mean in the first sentence?
choices: A person who visits a club
A home job that someone needs to do
questions.rationale: The word meaning comes from how it is used in the passage.

questions.prompt: Why did Yuna switch to job cards on a ring?
choices: The first tool was missing from the class
She thought a smaller tool would help her follow the order
The teacher told the student to stop working
The group wanted to decorate the room more
questions.rationale: The student changed after seeing which method worked better.
